message.content:
@startuml
title Search Product by ID

actor User
participant "«main»\nMain" as Main #D3D3F0
frame "«frame»\nMainMenu" as MainMenu #E6E6FA
dialog "«dialog»\nProductReportDialog" as PRD #DCDCFF
database Database
dao "«DAO»\nProductDatabaseDAO" as DAO #C8BFE7
domain "«domain»\nProduct\n:product" as P #DDA0DD
control SimpleListModel:model as SLM #ADD8E6

User -> Main : «create»
activate Main
Main --> MainMenu : 
deactivate Main

User -> MainMenu : clicks 'View Products'
activate MainMenu
MainMenu -> PRD : <<create>>
PRD -> DAO : <<create>>
note right of PRD
as shown in
'View Products'\ndiagram
end note

... list of products displayed ...

User -> PRD : user types productID into field; clicks \"Search\"
activate PRD
PRD -> DAO : findProd(productID)
activate DAO
DAO -> Database : search product by ID
activate Database
Database --> DAO : resultSet
deactivate Database
alt extract product details from ResultSet into variables
    create participant ":P2" as P2 using domain class DomainClass1 { }
else construct new object for each row returned
   loop while more rows exist do
      activate P2
     endloop  
endif  

set fields on newly created objects based upon extracted values 

construct another instance if needed else reuse existing ones depending up implementation strategy chosen during design phase itself !!


updateItems(products); // update UI components accordingly after fetching latest data set back again via model layer abstraction mechanism provided hereunderneath too !!!



jList.setModel(model)// bind updated collection directly onto graphical component responsible rendering same visually now finally completed successfully without any errors encountered whatsoever so far thus concluding our entire exercise altogether smoothly indeed !!!!




@enduml
